questions.question: Define a sector of a circle and explain its types.
questions.answer: A sector of a circle is the region enclosed by two radii and the corresponding arc. There are two types of sectors: minor sector, which is the smaller region, and major sector, which is the larger region.

questions.question: Explain what is meant by a segment of a circle.
questions.answer: A segment of a circle is the region enclosed by a chord and the corresponding arc. It is classified into minor segment and major segment.

questions.question: Explain the terms radius, diameter and chord of a circle.
questions.answer: The radius is the distance from the centre to the circle. The diameter is twice the radius. A chord is a line joining any two points on the circle.

questions.question: What is a quadrant? Explain with a diagram description.
questions.answer: A quadrant is one-fourth of a circle formed by two perpendicular radii making an angle of 90 degrees at the centre.

questions.question: Explain the meaning of arc of a circle.
questions.answer: An arc is a part of the circumference of a circle. It may be a minor arc or a major arc.

questions.question: Define circumference of a circle.
questions.answer: The circumference of a circle is the distance around the circle. It is given by the formula 2πr.

questions.question: Explain the formula for the area of a circle.
questions.answer: The area of a circle is given by πr², where r is the radius of the circle.

questions.question: What is meant by minor and major segments?
questions.answer: The smaller region formed by a chord is called the minor segment, while the larger region is called the major segment.

questions.question: Explain the term central angle.
questions.answer: A central angle is the angle subtended by an arc at the centre of the circle.

questions.question: State the value of π and explain why approximations are used.
questions.answer: π is an irrational number. Its value is approximately taken as 22/7 or 3.14 for calculations.

questions.question: Explain what is meant by a semicircle.
questions.answer: A semicircle is half of a circle formed by a diameter, subtending an angle of 180 degrees at the centre.

questions.question: Explain why every diameter is a chord.
questions.answer: A diameter joins two points on the circle and hence satisfies the definition of a chord.

questions.question: Explain why every chord is not a diameter.
questions.answer: A chord need not pass through the centre of the circle, whereas a diameter always does.

questions.question: Explain the unit of area used in circle problems.
questions.answer: Area is measured in square units such as cm² or m².

questions.question: Explain what happens to the area of a circle when radius increases.
questions.answer: The area of a circle increases as the square of the radius.

questions.question: Explain the relationship between radius and diameter.
questions.answer: The diameter of a circle is twice its radius.

questions.question: What is the angle of a full circle?
questions.answer: The angle of a full circle at the centre is 360 degrees.

questions.question: Explain the meaning of area in geometry.
questions.answer: Area is the measure of the region enclosed by a closed figure.

questions.question: Explain why the area of a circle with radius zero is zero.
questions.answer: When radius is zero, there is no enclosed region, so area is zero.

questions.question: Explain the term minor arc.
questions.answer: The smaller arc between two points on a circle is called the minor arc.

questions.question: Explain the term major arc.
questions.answer: The larger arc between two points on a circle is called the major arc.

questions.question: Explain why a circle has infinite points on its boundary.
questions.answer: A circle is a continuous curve, hence it contains infinitely many points.

questions.question: Explain what is meant by centre of a circle.
questions.answer: The centre is the point equidistant from all points on the circle.

questions.question: Explain the role of radius in circle formulas.
questions.answer: The radius determines both area and circumference of a circle.

questions.question: Summarise basic terms related to circles.
questions.answer: Important terms include radius, diameter, chord, arc, sector, segment and circumference.
